date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

houseSystem.description: House system used to place the Sun, which determines the chart sect (day when the Sun is above the horizon, night when below) and therefore which lot formula applies. Placidus (default), Whole Sign, Equal, or Koch.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the birth moment and place used to compute every lot.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

lots.description: The seven Hermetic lots in canonical order: Part of Fortune and Part of Spirit from the luminaries, then Eros, Necessity, Courage, Victory, and Nemesis from a planet paired with Fortune or Spirit.

degree.description: Degree of the lot within its zodiac sign (0 to 29.999).

formula.description: Human readable arc used for this chart, with the day or night term order already applied by sect.

id.description: Stable machine identifier for the lot (fortune, spirit, eros, necessity, courage, victory, nemesis). Use this for lookups; the name field carries the localized display label.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this lot in its sign, suitable for chart reports and AI agents. Localized to the requested language.

longitude.description: Absolute tropical ecliptic longitude of the lot in degrees (0 to 360).

name.description: Display name of the lot, localized to the requested language.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the lot falls in.

sect.description: Chart sect that selected each formula. Day (diurnal) when the Sun is above the horizon, night (nocturnal) when below. Every lot swaps its two non-Ascendant terms between day and night.

summary.description: Short overview of the lot set for previews and report intros.
summary.example: Seven Hermetic lots for 1990-07-15 cast from a night chart: Fortune, Spirit, Eros, Necessity, Courage, Victory, and Nemesis, each projected by arc from the Ascendant.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

AshtakavargaResponse.description: Complete Ashtakavarga analysis for a birth chart

bhinnashtakavarga.description: Individual planetary strength grids (Bhinnashtakavarga). Eight entries: one for each of the 7 classical planets plus Lagna. Each entry shows how many of the 8 contributors (7 planets + Lagna) give benefic points to that planet in each of the 12 signs.

items.description: Bhinnashtakavarga for a single planet or Lagna

bindus.description: Benefic points (bindus) for each of the 12 signs, ordered Aries through Pisces (index 0 = Aries, index 11 = Pisces). Each value ranges from 0 to 8, representing how many of the 8 contributors (7 planets + Lagna) provide a benefic point for this planet in that sign. Higher bindus indicate stronger planetary support.

planet.description: Planet or Lagna name. Seven classical planets (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn) plus Lagna (Ascendant). Rahu and Ketu are excluded from Ashtakavarga per BPHS.

total.description: Sum of bindus across all 12 signs. This total is constant per planet regardless of birth chart: Sun = 48, Moon = 49, Mars = 39, Mercury = 54, Jupiter = 56, Venus = 52, Saturn = 39, Lagna = 49. Useful as a validation checksum.

reducedBhinnashtakavarga.description: Reduced Bhinnashtakavarga after two-step Shodhana (purification) per BPHS Ch. 67-68. Step 1: Trikona Shodhana subtracts minimum bindu among trine groups (1-5-9, 2-6-10, 3-7-11, 4-8-12). Step 2: Ekadipati Shodhana adjusts dual-lordship sign pairs (Mars: Aries/Scorpio, Venus: Taurus/Libra, Mercury: Gemini/Virgo, Jupiter: Sagittarius/Pisces, Saturn: Capricorn/Aquarius). Used as input for Shodhya Pinda planetary strength.

items.description: Bhinnashtakavarga for a single planet or Lagna

bindus.description: Benefic points (bindus) for each of the 12 signs, ordered Aries through Pisces (index 0 = Aries, index 11 = Pisces). Each value ranges from 0 to 8, representing how many of the 8 contributors (7 planets + Lagna) provide a benefic point for this planet in that sign. Higher bindus indicate stronger planetary support.

planet.description: Planet or Lagna name. Seven classical planets (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn) plus Lagna (Ascendant). Rahu and Ketu are excluded from Ashtakavarga per BPHS.

total.description: Sum of bindus across all 12 signs. This total is constant per planet regardless of birth chart: Sun = 48, Moon = 49, Mars = 39, Mercury = 54, Jupiter = 56, Venus = 52, Saturn = 39, Lagna = 49. Useful as a validation checksum.

reducedSarvashtakavarga.description: Reduced Sarvashtakavarga. Sum of the 7 reduced planetary Bhinnashtakavarga values per sign (Lagna excluded). Indicates relative sign strength after Shodhana purification.

bindus.description: Combined benefic points per sign from all 7 planets (Lagna excluded from SAV), ordered Aries through Pisces. Higher values indicate stronger signs for transit predictions and house strength analysis. Average is approximately 28 per sign.

total.description: Sum of all SAV bindus across 12 signs. Always equals 337 for every birth chart. This mathematical constant serves as a validation checksum for the calculation.

sarvashtakavarga.description: Sarvashtakavarga (SAV) combining all 7 planetary Bhinnashtakavarga scores per sign. Total is always 337.

bindus.description: Combined benefic points per sign from all 7 planets (Lagna excluded from SAV), ordered Aries through Pisces. Higher values indicate stronger signs for transit predictions and house strength analysis. Average is approximately 28 per sign.

total.description: Sum of all SAV bindus across 12 signs. Always equals 337 for every birth chart. This mathematical constant serves as a validation checksum for the calculation.

shodhyaPinda.description: Shodhya Pinda planetary strength scores per BPHS Ch. 69. Derived from Reduced Ashtakavarga. Each entry contains Rashi Pinda (sign-weighted strength), Graha Pinda (planet-association-weighted strength), and total Shodhya Pinda. Used for comparing planetary strength, predicting dasha results, and transit analysis.

items.description: Shodhya Pinda strength values for a single planet, derived from Reduced Ashtakavarga per BPHS Ch. 69.

grahaPinda.description: Graha Pinda component. Weighted sum of reduced Bhinnashtakavarga bindus per sign multiplied by the Graha Gunakar of planets occupying each sign (Sun=5, Moon=5, Mars=8, Mercury=5, Jupiter=10, Venus=7, Saturn=5). Reflects planetary association strength.

planet.description: Planet or Lagna name. Shodhya Pinda is calculated for all 7 classical planets plus Lagna.

rashiPinda.description: Rashi Pinda component. Weighted sum of reduced Bhinnashtakavarga bindus per sign multiplied by Rashi Gunakar weights per BPHS Ch. 69. Higher values indicate stronger sign-based planetary strength.

shodhyaPinda.description: Total Shodhya Pinda (Rashi Pinda + Graha Pinda). Primary planetary strength score derived from Ashtakavarga reduction. Used for comparing relative strength of planets in a birth chart and predicting dasha period results.

signs.description: Sign names in order, for mapping bindus array indices to zodiac signs. Index 0 = Aries through index 11 = Pisces.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly.

options.description: Echo of the options used for this detection run. Useful for reproducibility and for downstream UI display.

include.description: Optional bodies included beyond the default Sun-Pluto set. Empty means classical 10-planet detection only.

strictOrbs.description: Whether the strict (Pontopia-style) orb budget was used. False uses industry-standard orbs (8 for major aspects, 9 for square, 6 for sextile, 3 for quincunx).

patterns.description: All aspect patterns detected in the chart, in detection order: Grand Cross first, then Kite, Grand Trine, T-Square, Yod, Mystic Rectangle, Stellium. Patterns absorbed by a higher-priority detection (T-Squares inside a Grand Cross, Grand Trines absorbed by a Kite) are not reported separately.

apex.description: Focal planet for Kite, T-Square, and Yod patterns. Receives the released energy of the configuration and is the recommended integration point.

dissociate.description: True if the pattern is out-of-sign (one or more planets in a neighboring element or modality). Dissociate patterns are still valid but operate with weakened thematic coherence.

element.description: Dominant element when the pattern is element-coherent (Grand Trine, Kite). Reported lowercase. Absent for patterns whose meaning does not pivot on element.

interpretation.description: Concise one-line interpretation naming the participating planets and theme. Localized to the requested language via the lang query parameter (defaults to English).
interpretation.example: Grand Trine in Water signs binds Sun, Moon, and Mars in flowing harmony, gifts that come easily but ask to be activated.

interpretationKey.description: Stable template identifier used to render the interpretation. Useful for clients that wish to swap in a custom narrative template while preserving the structured variables.

interpretationVars.description: Variables that were interpolated into the interpretation template. Names already resolved to the requested language where appropriate.

kind.description: Pattern kind identifier. GRAND_TRINE (3 trines, harmonious flow), KITE (Grand Trine with a focal outlet planet), T_SQUARE (opposition with squared apex, growth engine), GRAND_CROSS (4 planets in 2 oppositions and 4 squares, peak tension), YOD (Finger of Fate, fated adjustment), MYSTIC_RECTANGLE (oppositions softened by trines and sextiles), STELLIUM (3+ planets clustered in a sign or 10-degree arc).

modality.description: Dominant modality for tension-based patterns (T-Square, Grand Cross). Cardinal initiates, Fixed sustains, Mutable adapts.

name.description: Human-readable name of the configuration as used in astrological literature.

planets.description: Participating bodies in canonical order. For Kite, T-Square, and Yod the apex planet appears first.

tightness.description: Tightness score (0-100) derived from the average orb tightness across all defining aspects. Higher means closer to exact and stronger thematic expression.

total.description: Total number of detected aspect patterns in this chart.

aspectTypes.description: Optional: specific aspect types to find (defaults to all 9)

date.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format

planets.description: Optional: specific bodies to calculate aspects for (defaults to all 14: the 10 classical planets, the lunar nodes, Chiron, and Black Moon Lilith)

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours (NOT minutes format). Examples: New York EST = -5, India IST = 5.5 (NOT 5:30), Tokyo JST = 9. IMPORTANT: Use decimal format (5.5, not 5:30).

lines.description: One entry per body, each carrying its Midheaven, Imum Coeli, Ascendant, and Descendant planetary lines for relocation mapping.

ascendant.description: Ascendant (rising) line. Places where the body was on the eastern horizon, tied to identity, vitality, and self-expression.

circumpolarBeyond.description: Absolute latitude in degrees beyond which the body never crosses the horizon, so the line has no points past it. Null when the line spans the full sampled range.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this rising or setting planetary line for relocation, suitable for chart reports and AI agents.

points.description: Sampled geographic points tracing this rising or setting line from 70 South to 70 North. Join them in latitude order to draw the curved planetary line on a world map.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude of this sampled point in decimal degrees.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude in decimal degrees where the body sits exactly on the horizon at this latitude.

declination.description: Equatorial declination of the body in degrees (-90 to 90), which sets how far the rising and setting lines curve.

descendant.description: Descendant (setting) line. Places where the body was on the western horizon, tied to relationships and partnerships.

circumpolarBeyond.description: Absolute latitude in degrees beyond which the body never crosses the horizon, so the line has no points past it. Null when the line spans the full sampled range.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this rising or setting planetary line for relocation, suitable for chart reports and AI agents.

points.description: Sampled geographic points tracing this rising or setting line from 70 South to 70 North. Join them in latitude order to draw the curved planetary line on a world map.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude of this sampled point in decimal degrees.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude in decimal degrees where the body sits exactly on the horizon at this latitude.

ic.description: Imum Coeli (IC) line, opposite the MC. Places where the body was anti-culminating, tied to home, family, and inner foundations.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this meridian planetary line for relocation, suitable for chart reports and AI agents.

longitude.description: Constant geographic longitude of this vertical meridian line in decimal degrees. The body culminates (MC) or anti-culminates (IC) along it, so plot it as a straight north to south line.

mc.description: Midheaven (MC) line. Places along this meridian where the body was culminating overhead, tied to public life, career, and reputation.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this meridian planetary line for relocation, suitable for chart reports and AI agents.

longitude.description: Constant geographic longitude of this vertical meridian line in decimal degrees. The body culminates (MC) or anti-culminates (IC) along it, so plot it as a straight north to south line.

planet.description: Celestial body this set of planetary lines belongs to.

rightAscension.description: Equatorial right ascension of the body in degrees (0 to 360), the basis for every line.

symbol.description: Unicode astronomical symbol for this body.

summary.description: Short overview of the astrocartography map for previews and report intros.
summary.example: Astrocartography lines for 1990-07-15. 10 bodies, each with Midheaven, Imum Coeli, Ascendant, and Descendant lines mapping where their themes turn angular worldwide for relocation planning.

signs.description: Planets placed in this zodiac sign.

awastha.description: Baladi avastha, the planetary age-state set by the graha degree within its sign: Bala (infant), Kumara (child), Yuva (adult, strongest results), Vriddha (old), Mrita (dead, weakest). Bands run forward in odd signs and reversed in even signs. D1 birth chart only.

house.description: Bhava (house) number 1-12, counted whole-sign from the Lagna (house 1 is the Lagna rashi). Present on the D1 birth chart; divisional charts (navamsa, varga) omit it.

isRetrograde.description: True if planet is in retrograde motion (appears to move backward). Retrograde planets have altered significations.

longitude.description: Sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360) using Lahiri ayanamsa.

key.description: Nakshatra index (1-27) in the zodiac sequence starting from Ashwini.

lord.description: Vimshottari ruling planet of this nakshatra. One of the nine grahas (Ketu, Venus, Sun, Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury). Drives the dasha sequence and the nakshatra qualities.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion, 1 of 27) the planet occupies.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4). Each nakshatra has 4 padas of 3 degrees 20 each.

graha.description: Planet (graha) name. One of 9 Navagraha (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu) or Lagna (Ascendant). Used for matching transits and dasha lords to natal positions.

house.description: Bhava (house) number 1-12, counted whole-sign from the Lagna (house 1 is the Lagna rashi; Lagna itself is house 1). Present on the D1 birth chart; divisional charts omit it.

isRetrograde.description: True if the planet is in retrograde motion (appears to move backward through the zodiac). Retrograde planets carry intensified or internalized significations in Vedic interpretation.

longitude.description: Sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360) using Lahiri ayanamsa. Precise position used for aspect calculations, divisional chart mapping, and transit analysis.

key.description: Nakshatra sequence number (1-27) in zodiac order starting from Ashwini. Used for Tara Bala compatibility and dasha calculations.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the planet occupies. One of 27 Vedic nakshatras spanning 13 degrees 20 arcminutes each. Determines dasha lord and behavioral qualities.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4). Each nakshatra divides into 4 padas of 3 degrees 20 arcminutes. Pada determines Navamsa sign and refines personality traits.

rashi.description: Zodiac sign (rashi) the planet occupies in the birth chart. One of 12 Vedic rashis from Aries (Mesha) to Pisces (Meena).

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4). Each nakshatra has 4 padas of 3 degrees 20 each.

yogas.description: Twelve classical yogas detected against this chart: Gajakesari (three-rule parashara definition), Sunapha, Anapha, Dhurdhura, Kemadruma, Chandra Mangala, Budha-Aditya, and the five Pancha Mahapurusha (Ruchaka, Bhadra, Hamsa, Malavya, Sasa). Each entry carries an `id` (matches `GET /yoga/{id}` for full glossary lookup), a `present` boolean, a `quality` (Positive, Negative, or Both = auspicious, inauspicious, or context-dependent), and classical-text `evidence` for the rule that triggered or failed.

description.description: Brief classical formation rule. Identifies the planetary placement, lordship, dignity, or aspect pattern required for the yoga to form.
description.example: Jupiter in kendra from Moon

evidence.description: Human-readable rationale naming the specific rule that triggered or failed the detection, including planetary positions, dignity, kendrādhipati status, lordship, or malefic drishti.
evidence.example: Jupiter in kendra from Moon, not retrograde, no malefic drishti. Strengtheners: Moon out of 6/8/12 (house 4); Moon 5 houses from Sun; Moon not in Scorpio.

id.description: Glossary id (lowercase, kebab-case) matching an entry in the 300-entry planetary-yoga catalog. Use with GET /yoga/{id} to retrieve the full glossary text.

name.description: Classical Sanskrit name of the yoga as referenced in BPHS (Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra), Phaladeepika, and B.V. Raman *Three Hundred Important Combinations*.

present.description: True if every classical condition for the yoga is satisfied by the given chart. False if any rule fails, including "almost-present" cases where dignity is met but kendra/aspect is not.

quality.description: Overall nature. Auspicious yogas (Pancha Mahapurusha, Gajakesari) bestow benefits; inauspicious yogas (Kemadruma) indicate challenges; Both denotes context-dependent effects.

result.description: Classical phala (life-effect) description of the yoga when present, sourced from the parashari and phaladeepika tradition.
result.example: Gajakesari Yoga is one of the most powerful yogas...

arcana.description: Whether this card belongs to the Major Arcana (22 trump cards representing major life themes) or Minor Arcana (56 suit cards for daily situations).

id.description: Unique card identifier in kebab-case (e.g. fool, ace-of-cups, queen-of-swords).

imageUrl.description: URL to the tarot card artwork image in the Rider-Waite-Smith style.

keywords.description: Keywords for both upright and reversed orientations of this tarot card, useful for quick divination reference.

reversed.description: Key themes when the card is drawn reversed (inverted). Reversed meanings often indicate blocked or internalized energy.

upright.description: Key themes when the card is drawn upright. Used for quick tarot reference and reading summaries.

name.description: Display name of the tarot card as it appears in the Rider-Waite-Smith tradition.

number.description: Card number within its arcana. Major Arcana: 0 (Fool) through 21 (World). Minor Arcana: 1 (Ace) through 14 (King).

reversed.description: Complete reversed (inverted) interpretation including description, keywords, and guidance across love, career, finances, health, and spirituality domains. Reversed cards carry modified or blocked energy.

career.description: Career and professional interpretation for this orientation. Covers workplace dynamics, job transitions, ambition, and vocational purpose.

description.description: Full narrative interpretation of the card in this orientation. Covers symbolism, life lessons, and guidance for the querent.

finances.description: Financial interpretation for this orientation. Covers money management, investments, material prosperity, and abundance mindset.

health.description: Health and wellbeing interpretation for this orientation. Covers physical vitality, mental health, energy levels, and self-care guidance.

keywords.description: Key themes and concepts for this card in the given orientation (upright or reversed). Used for quick tarot reference and divination summaries.

love.description: Love and relationship interpretation for this orientation. Covers romantic partnerships, dating, emotional connections, and matters of the heart.

spirituality.description: Spiritual interpretation for this orientation. Covers personal growth, inner wisdom, soul purpose, and metaphysical development.

suit.description: Suit of the card (Minor Arcana only). Cups=emotions, Wands=creativity, Swords=intellect, Pentacles=material. Null for Major Arcana cards.

upright.description: Complete upright interpretation including description, keywords, and guidance across love, career, finances, health, and spirituality domains.

career.description: Career and professional interpretation for this orientation. Covers workplace dynamics, job transitions, ambition, and vocational purpose.

description.description: Full narrative interpretation of the card in this orientation. Covers symbolism, life lessons, and guidance for the querent.

finances.description: Financial interpretation for this orientation. Covers money management, investments, material prosperity, and abundance mindset.

health.description: Health and wellbeing interpretation for this orientation. Covers physical vitality, mental health, energy levels, and self-care guidance.

keywords.description: Key themes and concepts for this card in the given orientation (upright or reversed). Used for quick tarot reference and divination summaries.

love.description: Love and relationship interpretation for this orientation. Covers romantic partnerships, dating, emotional connections, and matters of the heart.

spirituality.description: Spiritual interpretation for this orientation. Covers personal growth, inner wisdom, soul purpose, and metaphysical development.

meaning.description: What the line statement asks of the querent, read from its position in the hexagram (1 is the hidden beginning, 3 is the exposed threshold, 5 is the ruling line, 6 is past the peak) and from whether the line is yin or yang. This is the meaning behind the image, so a consuming agent does not have to invent one.
meaning.example: The first line is the beginning, still underground and unrecognized. Strength is present but untested, and showing it now invites resistance it cannot yet survive. Stay hidden and build.

position.description: Line position (1-6, bottom to top). In I-Ching, each hexagram has six lines (yao) read from bottom upward.

text.description: The oracle statement for this line. It applies when this specific line comes up changing (old yin or old yang) in a casting, and it speaks in the concrete imagery of the tradition.
text.example: The dragon is still hidden below the surface, so it does not act.

person1.description: Birth data of the first person (typically the boy/groom in traditional Ashtakoot matching). Date, time, and location determine Moon nakshatra for koota scoring.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

person2.description: Birth data of the second person (typically the girl/bride in traditional Ashtakoot matching). Moon nakshatra compared against person 1 across all 8 kootas.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

breakdown.description: Detailed breakdown of compatibility scores across all 8 Ashtakoot kootas. Each category evaluates a different aspect of marital compatibility: temperament, physical, mental, financial, and health.

category.description: One of 8 Ashtakoot matching categories: Varna, Vashya, Tara, Yoni, Graha Maitri, Gana, Bhakoot, Nadi.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this koota category evaluates.
description.example: Spiritual compatibility and mutual respect

maxScore.description: Maximum possible points for this koota category.

person1.description: Classification of person 1 for this koota (e.g. Vaishya for Varna, Chatushpada for Vashya, Sheep for Yoni).

person2.description: Classification of person 2 for this koota.

score.description: Points scored in this category. Maximum varies: Varna (1), Vashya (2), Tara (3), Yoni (4), Graha Maitri (5), Gana (6), Bhakoot (7), Nadi (8).

doshaCancellations.description: Doshas detected but cancelled by classical exception rules. Nadi Dosha cancels when partners share the same Moon sign with different nakshatras, same nakshatra with different padas, or same nakshatra spanning different signs. Bhakoot Dosha cancels when Moon sign lords are the same planet or mutual natural friends. Koota score remains 0 but the dosha is not counted against the recommendation.

dosha.description: Name of the cancelled dosha (Nadi Dosha or Bhakoot Dosha).

reason.description: Classical cancellation condition that neutralizes this dosha. Based on Muhurta Martanda for Nadi Dosha and BPHS for Bhakoot Dosha.
reason.example: Moon sign lords are the same planet (Venus)

doshas.description: List of active (uncancelled) doshas in the matching. Doshas that meet classical cancellation conditions from Muhurta Martanda or BPHS are excluded from this array and appear in doshaCancellations instead. Common doshas: Nadi Dosha (same Nadi type, 0/8 points), Bhakoot Dosha (inauspicious Moon sign distance, 0/7 points). Empty array when no doshas are present or all detected doshas are cancelled.

isCompatible.description: True when percentage >= 50% (18/36 minimum). Based on the traditional Ashtakoot Gun Milan threshold used by Vedic astrologers for kundli matching.

maxScore.description: Maximum possible Guna Milan score (always 36). The 36 points are distributed across 8 kootas (matching categories).

percentage.description: Compatibility percentage derived from total/maxScore. Above 50% (18/36) is the traditional minimum threshold for marriage compatibility.

recommendation.description: Human-readable marriage recommendation based on overall score and dosha analysis. Indicates whether the union is recommended, and if not, specifies the reason (e.g. Nadi Dosha, Bhakoot Dosha, low overall score).

total.description: Total Ashtakoot Gun Milan score out of 36. Scores above 18 are considered compatible for marriage. Higher scores indicate stronger marital harmony.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

division.description: Divisional chart number. Each division reveals a specific life area. Supported: 2 (Hora, wealth), 3 (Drekkana, siblings), 4 (Chaturthamsa, property), 7 (Saptamsa, children), 9 (Navamsa, marriage), 10 (Dasamsa, career), 12 (Dwadasamsa, parents), 16 (Shodasamsa, vehicles), 20 (Vimsamsa, spirituality), 24 (Chaturvimsamsa, education), 27 (Bhamsa, strength), 30 (Trimsamsa, misfortunes), 40 (Khavedamsa, merit), 45 (Akshavedamsa, character), 60 (Shashtiamsa, past life karma).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

chart.description: Divisional chart showing planetary positions across 12 rashi houses plus a meta lookup. Same structure as birth chart and navamsa responses.

aries.description: One of the 12 divisional rashi-house buckets (aries shown; taurus through pisces follow the identical shape). Each lists the planets placed in that sign.

signs.description: Planets placed in this divisional sign.

graha.description: Planet (graha) placed in this divisional sign.

house.description: Bhava (house) number 1-12 in this divisional chart, counted whole-sign from the divisional Lagna.

isRetrograde.description: True if the planet is in retrograde motion.

longitude.description: Original sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360), same as the D1 birth chart. Preserved for cross-chart reference.

nakshatra.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) data for this planet, carried over from the D1 chart.

key.description: Nakshatra index in the zodiac sequence starting from Ashwini.

lord.description: Vimshottari ruling planet of this nakshatra.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the planet occupies.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4).

graha.description: Planet (graha) name. One of 9 Navagraha (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu) or Lagna (Ascendant). Used to match transits and dasha lords to divisional chart placements.

house.description: Bhava (house) number 1-12 in this divisional chart, counted whole-sign from the divisional Lagna. Specific to this varga and differs from the D1 birth-chart house.

isRetrograde.description: True if the planet is in retrograde motion (appears to move backward through the zodiac). Retrograde planets carry intensified or internalized significations in Vedic interpretation.

longitude.description: Original sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360) using Lahiri ayanamsa, same as D1 birth chart. Sign placement changes per division but longitude is preserved for cross-chart reference.

nakshatra.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) data for this planet. Nakshatras are the 27-fold division of the zodiac central to Vedic timing and compatibility systems.

key.description: Nakshatra sequence number (1-27) in zodiac order starting from Ashwini. Used for Tara Bala compatibility and dasha calculations.

lord.description: Vimshottari ruling planet of this nakshatra. One of the nine grahas (Ketu, Venus, Sun, Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury). Carried over from the D1 nakshatra.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the planet occupies. One of 27 Vedic nakshatras spanning 13 degrees 20 arcminutes each. Determines dasha lord and behavioral qualities.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4). Each nakshatra divides into 4 padas of 3 degrees 20 arcminutes. Pada determines Navamsa sign and refines personality traits.

rashi.description: Zodiac sign (rashi) the planet occupies in this divisional chart. May differ from the D1 birth chart sign. Comparing D1 and divisional rashi reveals Vargottama status and domain-specific strengths.

meta.description: Planet positions in the divisional chart keyed by planet name. Contains all 9 Navagraha plus Lagna.

division.description: Metadata about the selected divisional chart.

degreesPerDivision.description: Size of each division segment within a 30-degree sign.

name.description: English name of the divisional chart.

number.description: Division number (e.g. 10 for D10 Dasamsa).

sanskritName.description: Sanskrit name of the divisional chart.

significance.description: Life areas this divisional chart reveals.
significance.example: Career, profession, public reputation, and social status

vargottama.description: Planets that are Vargottama (same sign in D1 and this divisional chart). Vargottama planets deliver strong, consistent results.

arcana.description: Whether this card belongs to the Major Arcana (22 trump cards, major life themes) or Minor Arcana (56 suit cards, daily situations).

career.description: Career and professional interpretation for the drawn orientation. Covers workplace dynamics, job transitions, ambition, and vocational purpose.

finances.description: Financial interpretation for the drawn orientation. Covers money management, investments, material prosperity, and abundance mindset.

health.description: Health and wellbeing interpretation for the drawn orientation. Covers physical vitality, mental health, energy levels, and self-care guidance.

id.description: Unique card identifier in kebab-case (e.g. the-fool, ace-of-cups).

imageUrl.description: URL to the tarot card artwork image.

keywords.description: Key themes and concepts associated with this card in its current orientation (upright or reversed).

love.description: Love and relationship interpretation for the drawn orientation. Covers romantic partnerships, dating, emotional connections, and matters of the heart.

meaning.description: Full interpretation of this card in its current orientation, providing detailed divination guidance.

name.description: Display name of the tarot card.

number.description: Card number within its arcana. Major Arcana: 0 (Fool) through 21 (World). Minor Arcana: 1 (Ace) through 14 (King). Null when not applicable.

position.description: Position index of this card in the draw sequence (1-based). Useful for mapping cards to spread positions.

reversed.description: True if the card was drawn reversed (upside down). Reversed cards carry modified or blocked energy compared to upright position.

spirituality.description: Spiritual interpretation for the drawn orientation. Covers personal growth, inner wisdom, soul purpose, and metaphysical development.

suit.description: Suit of the card (Minor Arcana only). Cups=emotions, Wands=creativity, Swords=intellect, Pentacles=material. Null for Major Arcana cards.

id.description: Unique symbol identifier in kebab-case. Use this to fetch full interpretation via /symbols/{id}.

letter.description: Starting letter (a-z) for alphabetical dream dictionary navigation.

meaning.description: Full psychological dream interpretation explaining the subconscious symbolism, emotional significance, and waking-life connections of this dream symbol.
meaning.example: To see a snake in your dream signifies hidden fears and worries that are threatening you. Your dream may be alerting you to something in your waking life that you are not aware of or that has not yet surfaced.

name.description: Display name of the dream symbol.

ErrorResponse.description: Error body returned by every RoxyAPI endpoint on a 4xx or 5xx response.

allow.description: Present on 405 responses: the HTTP methods this path accepts.

code.description: Stable machine readable error code, for example validation_error or rate_limit_exceeded.

docs.description: Link to the documentation for this domain, when available.

error.description: Human readable error message. Wording may change; switch on code instead.

issues.description: Present on 400 responses: every field that failed validation.

ErrorResponseIssue.description: A single field-level validation failure from a 400 response.

code.description: Validation issue code, for example invalid_type or too_small.

expected.description: Expected type, when the issue is a type mismatch.

message.description: Human readable description of this failure.

path.description: Dot-separated field path, or (root) for a top-level error.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the birth moment and place used to precess every star.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

conjunctions.description: Flat list of every star to natal point conjunction, sorted tightest first, each with an interpretation. The high-value summary of where fixed stars touch the chart.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this star contact, blending the star traditional nature with the chart point. Localized to the requested language.

orb.description: Angular separation in degrees between the star and the natal point.

point.description: Natal point conjunct the star: a localized planet name, or the chart angles MC and ASC.

star.description: Proper name of the conjunct fixed star.

orb.description: Conjunction orb in degrees applied to detect contacts between stars and natal points.

stars.description: Every catalog star with its precessed tropical position, magnitude, traditional nature, and any conjunctions to the natal chart.

conjunctions.description: Natal points within the chosen orb of this star. Empty when no planet or angle contacts the star.

orb.description: Angular separation in degrees between the star and the natal point. Smaller means a tighter, stronger contact.

point.description: Natal point conjunct this star: a planet name, or the chart angles MC and ASC. Planet names are localized to the requested language.

pointLongitude.description: Tropical ecliptic longitude of the natal point in degrees (0-360).

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign (0-29.999).

id.description: Lowercase identifier for the fixed star.

keywords.description: Traditional astrological keywords associated with the star.

longitude.description: Tropical ecliptic longitude of the star in degrees (0-360), precessed from its J2000 position to the chart date.

magnitude.description: Apparent visual magnitude. Lower is brighter, and the brightest stars are negative.

name.description: Proper name of the fixed star.

nature.description: Traditional planetary nature of the star in classical astrology.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the star currently occupies.

summary.description: Short overview of the fixed-star report for previews and report intros.
summary.example: Fixed star positions for 1990-07-15 with 3 conjunctions within 1 degrees across the major named stars including Regulus, Spica, and Algol.

binary.description: Binary line pattern (6 digits, bottom to top). 1 = yang (solid line), 0 = yin (broken line). Lines 1-3 form the lower trigram, lines 4-6 form the upper trigram.

changingLines.description: Changing line interpretations for all 6 lines

chinese.description: Original Chinese character name of the hexagram in traditional script.

english.description: English translation of the hexagram name, conveying the core concept and life situation it represents.

image.description: The Image (Xiang) text, symbolic guidance derived from the trigram combination describing the ideal attitude and action.
image.example: Heaven above heaven: the same motion repeated without pause and without fatigue. Renew your own strength on your own schedule rather than waiting to be driven by circumstances.

judgment.description: The Judgment (Tuan) text, the primary oracle statement of the hexagram offering core guidance and outcome.
judgment.example: Pure creative force is available and it runs from origin to completion without obstruction, so the situation rewards initiative that stays correct and does not slacken. Success here is built by sustained effort held to a straight line, not by a single burst.

lowerTrigram.description: Lower trigram (lines 1-3). Combines with the upper trigram to form the hexagram and its meaning.

number.description: Hexagram number in the traditional King Wen sequence (1-64), the standard ordering used in I-Ching divination for over 3,000 years.

pinyin.description: Pinyin romanization of the Chinese name with tone marks for correct pronunciation.

symbol.description: Unicode hexagram symbol (U+4DC0 block) representing all six lines. Use for visual display in I-Ching apps and divination interfaces.

upperTrigram.description: Upper trigram (lines 4-6). One of 8 trigrams: Heaven, Earth, Thunder, Wind, Water, Fire, Mountain, Lake.

ascendant.description: Ascendant (rising sign) position. The eastern horizon point at the moment of birth, defining personality expression and physical appearance in Western astrology.

degree.description: Degree within the Ascendant sign (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of the Ascendant in degrees (0-360).

sign.description: Zodiac sign on the Ascendant (rising sign). Determines the first house cusp.

comparison.description: Side-by-side house cusp comparison across all four systems (Placidus, Whole Sign, Equal, Koch). Only included when houseSystem is set to "all". Useful for educational tools and system comparison.

date.description: Input date used for this house cusp calculation.

houseSystem.description: House system used for this calculation (placidus, whole-sign, equal, koch, or all).

houses.description: All 12 house cusps with their zodiac positions. House cusps divide the chart into life areas: identity (1st), resources (2nd), communication (3rd), home (4th), creativity (5th), health (6th), partnerships (7th), transformation (8th), philosophy (9th), career (10th), community (11th), spirituality (12th).

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign on this cusp (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Ecliptic longitude of this house cusp in degrees (0-360).

number.description: House number (1-12). Each house governs specific life areas.

sign.description: Zodiac sign on this house cusp.

latitude.description: Observer latitude used for horizon-based house calculations.

longitude.description: Observer longitude used for local sidereal time.

midheaven.description: Midheaven (MC) position. The highest point of the ecliptic at birth, representing career aspirations and public image in natal astrology.

degree.description: Degree within the Midheaven sign (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of the Midheaven in degrees (0-360).

sign.description: Zodiac sign on the Midheaven (MC). Indicates career direction and public reputation.

time.description: Input time used for this house cusp calculation.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC applied to this calculation.

advice.description: Practical wisdom and actionable advice from this hexagram for daily life application.
advice.example: Be like the heavens: consistent, powerful, and untiring. Make yourself strong through steady effort.

career.description: Career and professional life interpretation.
career.example: Exceptional opportunities for advancement and recognition. Your creative ideas are at their peak.

decision.description: Decision-making guidance for whether to act, wait, retreat, or advance based on this hexagram.
decision.example: The time is right for bold action. Trust your instincts and move forward with confidence.

general.description: General life situation interpretation of this hexagram.
general.example: This hexagram represents the primal power of creation and the energy of pure initiative.

love.description: Love and relationship guidance from this hexagram.
love.example: A time of strong attraction and passionate connection. Take the initiative in expressing your feelings.

calculated.description: UTC timestamp when calculation was performed

type.description: Ayanamsa type identifier

"lahiri" uses Lahiri/Chitrapaksha ayanamsa matching most traditional Vedic software. "custom" allows providing your own value via ayanamsaValue. Defaults to "kp-newcomb".

"true" uses the osculating node with perturbation corrections, oscillating up to 1.5 degrees from mean with a 173-day period. Impacts KP sub-lord assignments in narrow boundary cases. Defaults to "mean".

endDatetime.description: End datetime in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Maximum 7 days from start. Interpreted as local time when a non-zero timezone is provided (a trailing Z is accepted but ignored); with timezone 0 it is UTC.

intervalMinutes.description: Time between calculations in minutes. Range: 15 (quarter-hourly) to 1440 (daily).

latitude.description: Observer latitude in decimal degrees (for future Lagna calculations)

longitude.description: Observer longitude in decimal degrees (for future Lagna calculations)

nodeType.description: Lunar node type for Rahu and Ketu positions. "mean" uses the smooth mean node (traditional Vedic astrology default). "true" uses the osculating node with perturbation corrections, oscillating up to 1.5 degrees from mean with a 173-day period. Impacts KP sub-lord assignments in narrow boundary cases. Defaults to "mean".

startDatetime.description: Start datetime in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Interpreted as local time when a non-zero timezone is provided (a trailing Z is accepted but ignored); with timezone 0 it is UTC.

timezone.description: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC. IANA resolved to the DST-correct offset for the startDatetime date. When non-zero, all datetimes are treated as local time in this timezone (Z suffix is ignored). Defaults to 0 (UTC).

ayanamsa.description: Ayanamsa system used for this calculation. "kp-newcomb" = KP-Newcomb (dynamic), "kp-old" = Krishnamurti original (constant rate), "lahiri" = Lahiri/Chitrapaksha.

endDatetime.description: End of the KP ephemeris interval range (ISO 8601).

intervalMinutes.description: Time gap between consecutive planetary snapshots in minutes. Determines the granularity of the KP transit table.

intervals.description: Array of planetary snapshots at each time interval

datetime.description: Full datetime for this data point. Adjusted to requested timezone.

isRetrograde.description: True if the planet is in retrograde (vakri) motion. Rahu and Ketu are always retrograde. Retrograde planets deliver results differently in KP analysis.

ayanamsa.description: Ayanamsa system for sidereal conversion. "kp-newcomb" uses the KP-Newcomb dynamic formula, the most common choice for KP astrology. "kp-old" uses the Krishnamurti original table from KP Reader-1 with constant precession rate. "lahiri" uses Lahiri/Chitrapaksha ayanamsa, matching most traditional Vedic software. Defaults to "kp-newcomb".

endDate.description: End date for sign ingress search (YYYY-MM-DD format)

nodeType.description: Lunar node type for Rahu and Ketu positions. "mean" uses the smooth mean node (traditional Vedic astrology default). "true" uses the osculating node with perturbation corrections, oscillating up to 1.5 degrees from mean with a 173-day period. Impacts KP sub-lord assignments in narrow boundary cases. Defaults to "mean".

planet.description: Planet to track (case-insensitive). Valid values: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn

toSign.description: New zodiac sign entered by the planet. Marks the beginning of a new transit phase in Vedic gochar analysis.

dayLord.description: Ruling planet of the weekday based on Hindu sunrise Vara. Changes at local sunrise, not midnight.

lagnaSignLord.description: Lord of the Ascendant (Lagna) zodiac sign. Changes roughly every 2 hours as houses rotate.

lagnaStarLord.description: Lord of the nakshatra where the Ascendant degree falls.

lagnaSubSublord.description: KP sub-sublord of the Ascendant. Most granular level for pinpointing exact moments.

moonSignLord.description: Lord of the zodiac sign (rashi) where Moon is placed at this moment.

moonSignLordSignifies.description: KP 4-level significator breakdown for the Moon Sign Lord planet. Shows which houses the Moon rashi lord activates at this moment, broken down by strength tier.

L1.description: Level 1 (strongest signification). Houses influenced because this planet sits in the nakshatra (star) of a planet occupying those houses. For Rahu and Ketu, also includes L1 houses from agent planets (conjoined, aspecting, sign lord).

L2.description: Level 2. The house this planet physically occupies in the chart. For Rahu and Ketu, also includes houses occupied by agent planets (conjoined, aspecting, sign lord).

L3.description: Level 3. Houses influenced because this planet sits in the nakshatra of the sign lord (owner) of those houses. For Rahu and Ketu, also includes L3 houses from agent planets.

L4.description: Level 4 (weakest signification). Houses this planet rules by zodiac sign ownership. For Sun through Saturn, can be up to 2 houses. For Rahu and Ketu, includes houses owned by agent planets (conjoined, aspecting, sign lord).

moonSignifies.description: KP 4-level significator breakdown for Moon itself. Shows which bhavas Moon directly activates based on its position and star lord in the current moment chart.

fromNakshatraLord.description: Nakshatra lord (star lord) before transition. Follows the Vimshottari dasha sequence of 9 planets.

fromSublord.description: KP sublord planet before transition. The sublord determines whether an event signified by the star lord will manifest.

time.description: Precise sublord transition time (HH:MM, 24-hour). Refined via binary search to ~1 minute accuracy. Adjusted to requested timezone.

toKp.description: New KP number (1-249) the planet enters. Each number maps to a unique star lord and sublord combination.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the birth moment and place used to compute both Lilith variants.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

houseSystem.description: House system applied when placing each variant in a house.

lilith.description: Both Black Moon Lilith variants, the mean lunar apogee first and the true (osculating) apogee second.

degree.description: Degree of the apogee within its zodiac sign (0 to 29.999).

house.description: House placement (1 to 12) in the selected house system, the area of life where the Lilith theme plays out.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this Lilith variant in its sign, suitable for chart reports and AI agents. Localized to the requested language.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the apogee is moving backward. Always false for the mean apogee; the true apogee turns retrograde when the osculating ellipse swings the apogee direction backward.

latitude.description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees, the projection of the apogee off the ecliptic plane. Reaches up to about 5 degrees because the lunar orbit is inclined.

longitude.description: Absolute tropical ecliptic longitude of the apogee in degrees (0 to 360).

note.description: Short explanation of how this variant is defined and how it differs from the other, localized to the requested language.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the apogee falls in, naming where the suppressed instinct lives.

speed.description: Daily motion in degrees per day. The mean apogee is always positive (direct); the true apogee can be negative (retrograde).

variant.description: Which lunar apogee this entry describes, localized to the requested language. The mean variant is the smoothed average apogee; the true variant is the instantaneous osculating apogee.

summary.description: Short overview of both variants for previews and report intros.
summary.example: Black Moon Lilith for 1961-08-04: mean apogee in Leo and true osculating apogee in Leo, each with sign, degree, house, and interpretation.

birthDetails.description: The birthplace and birth instant the map was computed for.

date.description: Birth date echoed from the request.

latitude.description: Birthplace latitude, the origin of every local space line.

longitude.description: Birthplace longitude, the origin of every local space line.

time.description: Birth time echoed from the request.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC applied to the birth instant.

bodies.description: Every requested body with its horizon direction, altitude, compass direction, great-circle line, and interpretation.

aboveHorizon.description: True when the body is above the local horizon (altitude greater than 0) at the birth moment.

altitude.description: Angular height of the body above (positive) or below (negative) the local horizon at birth, in degrees (-90 to 90).

azimuth.description: Compass bearing of the body as seen from the birthplace, in degrees clockwise from true north (0 = north, 90 = east, 180 = south, 270 = west). This is the direction the local space line points.

compassDirection.description: Nearest 16-point compass abbreviation for the azimuth (N, NNE, NE, ENE, E, ESE, SE, SSE, S, SSW, SW, WSW, W, WNW, NW, NNW).

interpretation.description: Plain-language reading of what travelling or facing along this body line tends to emphasize. Localized when a translation exists.
interpretation.example: Your Sun line points southeast from your birthplace. Travelling or facing this direction tends to emphasize Self-awareness and ego in your daily experience.

line.description: The great-circle directional line for this body, ready to plot on a map.

points.description: Ordered latitude and longitude waypoints tracing the great-circle local space line from the birthplace along the body azimuth. The first point is the birthplace itself.

latitude.description: Waypoint latitude in decimal degrees.

longitude.description: Waypoint longitude in decimal degrees.

planet.description: Body name (Sun, Moon, Mercury through Pluto, plus North Node, Chiron, or Black Moon Lilith when requested). Localized when a translation exists.

symbol.description: Unicode astronomical symbol for this body.

summary.description: One-line overview of the local space map.
summary.example: Local space directional map of 10 celestial bodies seen from latitude 40.7128, longitude -74.006. 6 of them sit above the horizon at the birth moment.
