sign.description: Zodiac sign on this house cusp.

midheaven.description: Midheaven (MC). The highest point of the ecliptic at birth, representing career direction and public image.

degree.description: Degree within the Midheaven sign (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of the Midheaven (0-360).

sign.description: Zodiac sign on the Midheaven (MC).

partOfFortune.description: Part of Fortune (Lot of Fortune). A point derived from the Ascendant and the two luminaries that marks an area of ease, vitality, and material wellbeing in the chart.

degree.description: Degree within the Part of Fortune sign (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of the Part of Fortune (0-360).

sect.description: Chart sect used for the calculation. Day (diurnal) when the Sun is above the horizon, night (nocturnal) when below. Day charts use Ascendant plus Moon minus Sun, night charts use Ascendant plus Sun minus Moon.

sign.description: Zodiac sign holding the Part of Fortune.

patterns.description: Detected multi-planet aspect configurations (Grand Trine, Kite, T-Square, Grand Cross, Yod, Mystic Rectangle, Stellium). Grand Cross suppresses contained T-Squares, Kite suppresses underlying Grand Trine.

apex.description: Focal planet for Kite, T-Square, and Yod patterns. Receives the released energy of the configuration and is the recommended integration point.

dissociate.description: True if the pattern is out-of-sign (one or more planets in a neighboring element or modality). Dissociate patterns are still valid but operate with weakened thematic coherence.

element.description: Dominant element when the pattern is element-coherent (Grand Trine, Kite). Reported lowercase. Absent for patterns whose meaning does not pivot on element.

interpretation.description: Concise one-line interpretation naming the participating planets and theme. Localized to the requested language via the lang query parameter (defaults to English).
interpretation.example: Grand Trine in Water signs binds Sun, Moon, and Mars in flowing harmony, gifts that come easily but ask to be activated.

interpretationKey.description: Stable template identifier used to render the interpretation. Useful for clients that wish to swap in a custom narrative template while preserving the structured variables.

interpretationVars.description: Variables that were interpolated into the interpretation template. Names already resolved to the requested language where appropriate.

kind.description: Pattern kind identifier. GRAND_TRINE (3 trines, harmonious flow), KITE (Grand Trine with a focal outlet planet), T_SQUARE (opposition with squared apex, growth engine), GRAND_CROSS (4 planets in 2 oppositions and 4 squares, peak tension), YOD (Finger of Fate, fated adjustment), MYSTIC_RECTANGLE (oppositions softened by trines and sextiles), STELLIUM (3+ planets clustered in a sign or 10-degree arc).

modality.description: Dominant modality for tension-based patterns (T-Square, Grand Cross). Cardinal initiates, Fixed sustains, Mutable adapts.

name.description: Human-readable name of the configuration as used in astrological literature.

planets.description: Participating bodies in canonical order. For Kite, T-Square, and Yod the apex planet appears first.

tightness.description: Tightness score (0-100) derived from the average orb tightness across all defining aspects. Higher means closer to exact and stronger thematic expression.

planets.description: All 14 celestial bodies (10 classical planets, lunar nodes, Chiron, Black Moon Lilith) with zodiac signs, house placements, and interpretations.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign (0-29.999).

house.description: House placement (1-12) based on the selected house system.

interpretation.description: Planet-in-sign-in-house interpretation. Narrative analysis of what this placement means in the natal chart.

detailed.description: Multi-sentence detailed interpretation with personality insights.
detailed.example: Sun represents self-awareness and ego. In Cancer, this energy becomes nurturing, protective, emotionally intelligent...

keywords.description: Key personality traits and themes for this placement.

summary.description: One-sentence interpretation of this planet in its sign and house placement.
summary.example: Your Sun in Cancer in The Seventh House reveals how you express self-awareness and ego in the realm of partnerships.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the planet is in retrograde motion.

latitude.description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees.

longitude.description: Tropical ecliptic longitude in degrees (0-360).

name.description: Planet or point name (Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto, North Node, South Node, Chiron, Black Moon Lilith). The lunar nodes are the mean node; software using the true node may show node positions up to 1.75 degrees different.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign this planet occupies.

speed.description: Daily motion in degrees per day. Negative values indicate retrograde.

summary.description: Chart summary with dominant element, modality, retrograde planets, and distribution analysis.

dominantElement.description: Most represented element in the chart (Fire, Earth, Air, Water).

dominantModality.description: Most represented modality in the chart (Cardinal, Fixed, Mutable).

elementDistribution.description: Count of planets in each element. Shows elemental emphasis in the personality.

modalityDistribution.description: Count of planets in each modality. Shows the dominant operating mode.

retrogradePlanets.description: Planets in retrograde motion at the time of birth.

vertex.description: Vertex. The western intersection of the prime vertical with the ecliptic, often read as a point of fated encounters and turning-point relationships. The opposite point is the Anti-Vertex.

degree.description: Degree within the Vertex sign (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of the Vertex (0-360).

sign.description: Zodiac sign holding the Vertex.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

chart.description: Navamsa (D9) divisional chart showing planetary positions across 12 rashi houses plus a meta lookup. Same structure as the birth chart response.

aries.description: One of the 12 navamsa rashi-house buckets (aries shown; taurus through pisces follow the identical shape). Each lists the planets placed in that sign.

signs.description: Planets placed in this navamsa sign.

graha.description: Planet (graha) placed in this navamsa sign.

house.description: Bhava (house) number 1-12 in the Navamsa chart, counted whole-sign from the D9 Lagna.

isRetrograde.description: True if the planet is in retrograde motion.

longitude.description: Original sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360), same as the D1 birth chart. Preserved for cross-chart reference.

nakshatra.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) data for this planet, carried over from the D1 chart.

key.description: Nakshatra index in the zodiac sequence starting from Ashwini.

lord.description: Vimshottari ruling planet of this nakshatra.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the planet occupies.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4).

graha.description: Planet (graha) name. One of 9 Navagraha (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu) or Lagna (Ascendant). In Navamsa, Venus and Jupiter placements are especially significant for marriage and spiritual growth.

house.description: Bhava (house) number 1-12 in the Navamsa chart, counted whole-sign from the D9 Lagna. This is the Navamsa-specific house and differs from the D1 birth-chart house.

isRetrograde.description: True if the planet is in retrograde motion (appears to move backward through the zodiac). Retrograde planets carry intensified or internalized significations in Vedic interpretation.

longitude.description: Sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360) using Lahiri ayanamsa. Same as D1 birth chart longitude, preserved for cross-chart reference and aspect analysis.

nakshatra.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) data for this planet. Nakshatras are the 27-fold division of the zodiac central to Vedic timing and compatibility systems.

key.description: Nakshatra sequence number (1-27) in zodiac order starting from Ashwini. Used for Tara Bala compatibility and dasha calculations.

lord.description: Vimshottari ruling planet of this nakshatra. One of the nine grahas (Ketu, Venus, Sun, Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury). Carried over from the D1 nakshatra.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the planet occupies. One of 27 Vedic nakshatras spanning 13 degrees 20 arcminutes each. Determines dasha lord and behavioral qualities.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4). Each nakshatra divides into 4 padas of 3 degrees 20 arcminutes. Pada determines Navamsa sign and refines personality traits.

rashi.description: Zodiac sign (rashi) the planet occupies in the Navamsa (D9) chart. D9 sign placement reveals the deeper quality of a planet and is critical for spouse characteristics and marriage timing.

meta.description: Planet positions in the Navamsa (D9) chart keyed by planet name. Contains all 9 Navagraha plus Lagna.

vargottama.description: Planets that are Vargottama (same sign in D1 and D9)

vargottamaExplanation.example: Vargottama planets occupy the same zodiac sign in both D1 (birth chart) and D9 (Navamsa chart), indicating exceptional strength and purity. These planets deliver their full results with minimal affliction, bringing stability and success in their significations. Vargottama is considered highly auspicious, especially for benefics like Jupiter and Venus, as it doubles the positive effects in marriage, spirituality, and overall life prosperity.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

awastha.description: Baladi avastha, the planetary age-state set by the graha degree within its sign: Bala (infant), Kumara (child), Yuva (adult, strongest results), Vriddha (old), Mrita (dead, weakest). Bands run forward in odd signs and reversed in even signs.

combustionDistance.description: Angular distance from the Sun in degrees (0-180). Smaller values indicate closer proximity. Null for Sun, Rahu, Ketu, and Lagna. Useful for gauging combustion severity and planetary strength analysis.

graha.description: Vedic planet (graha) name. One of the Navagraha: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, or Lagna (Ascendant).

house.description: House number (1-12) the planet occupies using Whole Sign house system. House 1 is the Lagna (Ascendant) sign. Essential for bhava analysis and house-level predictions.

isCombust.description: Whether the planet is combust (asta, moudhya). A planet is combust when too close to the Sun, weakening its significations. Combustion orbs vary by planet: Moon 12 deg, Mars 17 deg, Mercury 14 deg (12 deg if retrograde), Jupiter 11 deg, Venus 10 deg (8 deg if retrograde), Saturn 15 deg. Sun, Rahu, Ketu, and Lagna are never combust. Based on Surya Siddhanta combustion orbs.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the planet is in retrograde motion (vakri). Rahu and Ketu are always retrograde in Vedic astrology.

latitude.description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees, the angular distance north (positive) or south (negative) of the ecliptic. Used in planetary war (graha yuddha) winner resolution and latitude-sensitive analysis. Omitted for the Lagna (Ascendant).

longitude.description: Sidereal longitude in degrees (0-360) using Lahiri ayanamsa. Precise planetary position for chart calculations.

nakshatra.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) data with optional interpretive details from Vedic tradition.

characteristics.description: Personality traits and behavioral tendencies when a planet occupies this nakshatra. Used for character analysis and prediction.

deity.description: Presiding deity of the nakshatra from Vedic mythology. Influences the spiritual quality and karmic themes of the planet placement.

key.description: Nakshatra sequence number (1-27) in zodiac order starting from Ashwini. Used for Tara Bala and dasha calculations.

name.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the planet occupies. One of 27 Vedic nakshatras spanning 13 degrees 20 arcminutes each.

pada.description: Nakshatra pada (quarter, 1-4). Each nakshatra divides into 4 padas of 3 degrees 20 arcminutes. Determines Navamsa sign.

symbol.description: Traditional symbol representing the nakshatra. Reflects core energy and life themes associated with this lunar mansion.

rashi.description: Zodiac sign (rashi) the planet occupies. One of 12 Vedic rashis from Aries to Pisces.

rashiDetails.description: Vedic zodiac sign (rashi) details including Sanskrit name, symbol, elemental energy, and personality characteristics. Present when interpretation data is available.

characteristics.description: Key personality traits and behavioral tendencies of this zodiac sign in Vedic astrology.

energy.description: Elemental and gender classification of the rashi (Masculine/Feminine, Fire/Earth/Air/Water).

symbol.description: Traditional symbol representing this zodiac sign.

vedicName.description: Sanskrit name of the zodiac sign as used in traditional Jyotish texts.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

houseSystem.description: House system used only to report where the lord of the year sits in the natal chart. The profected house and sign always use whole sign profection from the rising sign. Placidus (default), Whole Sign, Equal, or Koch.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

targetDate.description: Date whose profection year you want, in YYYY-MM-DD format. The completed whole years from the birth date to this date select the profected house and sign. Must fall on or after the birth date.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly.

age.description: Completed whole years from birth to the target date. Age 0 is the birth year and profects the first house.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the birth moment and place used to derive the natal Ascendant and the lord of the year placement.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

interpretation.description: Plain language reading of the profection year, combining the profected house theme, the profected sign, and the lord of the year. Localized to the requested language.

lordNatalPosition.description: Where the lord of the year sits in the birth chart: the natal sign and house that ground the theme of the profection year.

house.description: Natal house the lord of the year occupies (1 to 12), in the requested house system.

sign.description: Zodiac sign the lord of the year occupies in the natal chart.

lordOfYear.description: Lord of the year (annual time lord): the traditional ruling planet of the profected sign whose natal condition and transits color the themes of the year.

profectedHouse.description: Profected whole sign house activated for the year (1 to 12), computed as age modulo 12 plus 1. House 1 is the rising sign and the cycle repeats every twelve years.

profectedSign.description: Tropical zodiac sign on the profected house: the rising sign advanced by one whole sign for each completed year.

summary.description: Short overview of the profection year for previews and report intros.
summary.example: At age 30, the annual profection activates house 7 in Leo, making Sun the lord of the year for 2025-08-04.

targetDate.description: Target date whose profection year was computed, echoed from the request.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

targetDate.description: Date to progress the chart to, in YYYY-MM-DD format. Usually today or a forecast date. The day-for-a-year key turns the elapsed years since birth into the same number of ephemeris days after the birth moment.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly.

ascendant.description: Progressed Ascendant, the rising degree advanced by the Naibod arc per year. Marks the evolving outward style and immediate environment.

degree.description: Degree of the progressed angle within its zodiac sign (0 to 29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute tropical ecliptic longitude of the progressed angle in degrees (0 to 360).

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the progressed angle falls in.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the birth moment and place the progressed chart is built from.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

elapsedYears.description: Years elapsed between birth and the target date, measured in mean tropical years. Drives both the progressed planets and the Naibod progression of the angles. Negative values progress the chart converse (before birth).

midheaven.description: Progressed Midheaven, the culminating degree advanced by the Naibod arc per year. Marks the evolving vocation and public direction.

degree.description: Degree of the progressed angle within its zodiac sign (0 to 29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute tropical ecliptic longitude of the progressed angle in degrees (0 to 360).

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the progressed angle falls in.

planets.description: Every progressed body in canonical order: the 10 classical planets, the lunar nodes, Chiron, and Black Moon Lilith. The progressed Sun moves about one degree per year and the progressed Moon about one sign per two and a half years, so these two are the headline movers in any progressed reading.

degree.description: Degree of the progressed body within its zodiac sign (0 to 29.999).

house.description: Whole-sign house (1 to 12) counted from the progressed Ascendant sign. The 1st house is the entire sign the progressed Ascendant falls in.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this progressed body in its sign, suitable for chart reports and AI agents. Localized to the requested language.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the body is retrograde at the progressed instant, true when the daily speed is negative.

longitude.description: Progressed tropical ecliptic longitude in degrees (0 to 360).

name.description: Body name in canonical English. One of the 10 classical planets, the lunar nodes, Chiron, or Black Moon Lilith.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the progressed body falls in.

speed.description: Daily motion of the body at the progressed instant in degrees per day. Negative values indicate retrograde motion.

progressedDate.description: UTC calendar date of the progressed moment, the ephemeris day whose real positions stand in for the target date. One day after birth per year of elapsed life.

summary.description: Short overview of the progressed chart led by the progressed Sun and Moon. Localized to the requested language.
summary.example: Your secondary progressed chart advances the birth chart by one day for each year of life.

targetDate.description: The requested date the chart was progressed to.

characteristics.description: Key personality traits and behavioral tendencies of natives born under this rashi.
characteristics.example: Creative, idealistic in nature, headstrong, good leader

dateRange.description: Approximate sidereal date range when the Sun transits this rashi.

energy.description: Aditya (solar deity) governing this rashi in Vedic tradition.

id.description: Unique slug identifier for the rashi. Used in URL paths and cross-references.

name.description: Western zodiac sign name corresponding to this Vedic rashi.

symbol.description: Traditional symbol associated with this zodiac sign.

dateRange.description: Approximate sidereal date range when the Sun transits this rashi.

energy.description: Aditya (solar deity) governing this rashi in Vedic tradition.

birthLatitude.description: Birthplace latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Used for the original natal angles and houses that the relocated chart is compared against.

birthLongitude.description: Birthplace longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive East, negative West.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. The birth moment is unchanged by relocation, so this still defines the planetary positions of the chart.

houseSystem.description: House system for dividing the relocated chart into 12 houses. Placidus (default) is time-sensitive and most popular in Western astrology. Whole Sign assigns one sign per house. Equal divides into 30 degree segments from the Ascendant. Koch emphasizes higher latitudes. Quadrant systems fall back to Whole Sign above the polar circle.

relocationLatitude.description: New location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). The relocated Ascendant and house cusps are most sensitive to north-south movement.

relocationLongitude.description: New location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). The relocated Midheaven shifts roughly one degree per degree of longitude moved.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Combined with the timezone it fixes the exact birth instant, which the relocated angles and houses are recomputed for.

timezone.description: Birth timezone: decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 5.5 for IST) OR IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York"). Resolved to the DST-correct offset for the birth date. This is the birthplace timezone, not the new location timezone.

ascendant.description: Relocated Ascendant (rising sign). The eastern horizon at the new place, defining how you come across there.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign on this angle (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of this angle in degrees (0-360).

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign on this relocated angle.

birthDetails.description: Birthplace details echoed back. The birth instant is unchanged by relocation.

date.description: Birth date used for this chart (YYYY-MM-DD).

latitude.description: Birthplace latitude in decimal degrees.

longitude.description: Birthplace longitude in decimal degrees.

time.description: Birth time used for this chart (HH:MM:SS, 24-hour).

timezone.description: Birth timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours.

changes.description: How relocation reshapes the chart: Ascendant shift, planets that change house, angular planets, and the move geometry from the birthplace.

angularPlanets.description: Bodies within three degrees of a relocated angle (Ascendant, Imum Coeli, Descendant, or Midheaven), where their influence is strongest at this location.

ascendantSignChanged.description: Whether the Ascendant sign differs between the birthplace chart and the relocated chart.

direction.description: Compass direction (16-point) from the birthplace to the new location.

distanceKm.description: Great-circle distance from the birthplace to the new location in kilometers.

planetsChangedHouse.description: Bodies whose house placement shifts from the birthplace chart to the relocated chart. Empty when nothing changes house.

natalHouse.description: House this body occupied in the birthplace chart (1-12).

planet.description: Body that occupies a different house after relocation.

relocatedHouse.description: House this body occupies in the relocated chart (1-12).

houseSystem.description: House system used for the relocated chart (placidus, whole-sign, equal, or koch). Quadrant systems fall back to whole-sign above the polar circle.

houses.description: All 12 relocated house cusps with zodiac positions for the new location.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign on this cusp (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Ecliptic longitude of this house cusp in degrees (0-360).

number.description: House number (1-12). Each house governs specific life themes in Western astrology.

sign.description: Zodiac sign on this house cusp. Colors the themes of this life area.

interpretation.description: Relocation interpretation summary.

summary.description: Narrative summary of the relocation: the new Ascendant and Midheaven signs and any planets that move onto the angles. Localized via the lang query parameter.
summary.example: Relocated to this place, your chart angles shift while the planets keep their birth positions. The Ascendant moves to Gemini and the Midheaven to Aquarius, and Venus moves close to the angles, where its influence grows strongest in daily life here.

midheaven.description: Relocated Midheaven (MC). The highest point of the ecliptic at the new place, tied to career and public image there.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign on this angle (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of this angle in degrees (0-360).

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign on this relocated angle.

planets.description: All 14 celestial bodies with their unchanged natal signs and degrees, reassigned to the houses they occupy in the relocated chart.

relocation.description: New location the chart was recomputed for.

latitude.description: New location latitude in decimal degrees.

longitude.description: New location longitude in decimal degrees.

vertex.description: Relocated Vertex. The western prime-vertical and ecliptic intersection at the new place, read as a point of fated encounters.

degree.description: Degree within the Vertex sign (0-29.999).

longitude.description: Absolute ecliptic longitude of the Vertex (0-360).

sign.description: Zodiac sign holding the Vertex.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign (0-29.999). Indicates how far the planet has progressed through the sign.

house.description: House placement (1-12). Determined by the selected house system and birth location.

interpretation.description: Relocated placement interpretation. The planet keeps its natal sign, so this reads its meaning through the new house it occupies at this location.

detailed.description: Multi-sentence interpretation of this relocated placement.
detailed.example: Sun represents self-awareness and ego. In Leo, this energy becomes confident and expressive...

keywords.description: Key themes for this relocated placement.

summary.description: One-sentence interpretation of this planet in its sign and relocated house.
summary.example: Your Sun in Leo in The Third House reveals how you express self-awareness and ego in the realm of communication.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the planet appears to move backward from Earth perspective. Retrograde periods signal review and introspection.

latitude.description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees. Near zero for most planets, varies for the Moon and Pluto, and reaches up to about 5 degrees for Black Moon Lilith (projected from the inclined mean lunar orbit).

longitude.description: Tropical ecliptic longitude in degrees (0-360). Primary coordinate for zodiac sign and aspect calculations.

name.description: Body name. One of the 10 classical planets (Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto), the lunar nodes (North Node, South Node), Chiron, or Black Moon Lilith (the mean lunar apogee).

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign this planet occupies. Determined by 30-degree divisions of ecliptic longitude.

speed.description: Daily motion in degrees per day. Negative values indicate retrograde motion.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is CRITICAL for Lagna (Ascendant) calculation and house divisions. It changes every two hours roughly. Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

description.description: Human-readable Sadhesati analysis with current Saturn transit phase relative to natal Moon
description.example: Sadhesati active. Peak phase (1st house) - Saturn transiting relative to natal Moon.

effects.description: Sadhesati effects by transit phase with general and phase-specific impacts

general.description: Overall impact of Saturn transit during Sade Sati period
general.example: Overall period brings karmic lessons, maturity, and eventual growth through challenges

phases.description: Phase-specific effects for the current Sadhesati stage (Rising/Peak/Setting)

example.Peak phase (1st house): Health issues, relationship problems, career obstacles, maximum hardships

present.description: Whether Sade Sati is currently active, Saturn transiting 12th, 1st, or 2nd house from natal Moon

remedies.description: Traditional Vedic remedies for Shani Sade Sati including Shani mantras, donations, and worship
example: Light mustard oil lamp under peepal tree on Saturdays

severity.description: Sadhesati intensity: Moderate for Rising/Setting phases, Severe for Peak phase (Saturn over natal Moon)

type.description: Current Sadhesati phase: Rising (12th house), Peak (1st house), or Setting (2nd house)

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees. Location determines local sidereal time for Lagna calculation and affects bhava (house) cusps. Example: Delhi 28.6139, Mumbai 19.0760, Kathmandu 27.7172.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

ShadbalaResponse.description: Complete Shadbala (six-fold planetary strength) analysis for a birth chart per Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra (BPHS).

planets.description: Shadbala analysis for all 7 classical planets. Ordered: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn. Each entry contains all 6 strength components, total strength in virupas and Rupas, Ishta/Kashta Phala, minimum required threshold, strength ratio, and relative rank.

items.description: Shadbala (six-fold strength) analysis for a single planet with all components, totals, Ishta/Kashta Phala, and relative ranking.

chestaBala.description: Chesta Bala (Motional Strength) in virupas. Based on planetary motion and retrogression. Retrograde planets score higher. Sun uses Ayana Chesta Bala (declination arc), Moon uses elongation from Sun. Other planets use Sheeghrochcha (mean anomaly) from Surya Siddhanta elements. Range 0 to 60.

digBala.description: Dig Bala (Directional Strength) in virupas. Based on angular distance from the planets directional strength house. Sun and Mars are strong at MC (10th), Moon and Venus at IC (4th), Mercury and Jupiter at ASC (1st), Saturn at DSC (7th). Range 0 to 60.

drikBala.description: Drik Bala (Aspectual Strength) in virupas. Strength gained or lost from aspects received by other planets. Benefic aspects (Jupiter, Venus) add strength, malefic aspects (Sun, Mars, Saturn) reduce it. Can be negative when malefic aspects dominate. Uses Sputa Drishti with Vishesha (special) aspects for Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn.

ishtaPhala.description: Ishta Phala (auspicious strength) in virupas. Derived from Uchcha Bala and Chesta Bala: sqrt(ucchaBala * chestaBala). Indicates the planets capacity to produce favorable results during its dasha and transit periods.

kalaBala.description: Kala Bala (Temporal Strength) in virupas. Sum of 8 sub-components: Nathonnatha (day/night strength), Paksha (lunar phase), Tribhaga (third of day/night), Vara (weekday lord), Hora (planetary hour), Abda (year lord), Masa (month lord), and Ayana (declination-based seasonal strength).

kashtaPhala.description: Kashta Phala (malefic strength) in virupas. Derived from complements of Uchcha and Chesta Bala: sqrt((60 - ucchaBala) * (60 - chestaBala)). Indicates the planets capacity to produce unfavorable results. Zero when both Uchcha and Chesta exceed 60.

minRequired.description: Minimum required strength in Rupas per BPHS. Sun 5.0, Moon 6.0, Mars 5.0, Mercury 7.0, Jupiter 6.5, Venus 5.5, Saturn 5.0. A planet below its minimum is considered weak and may underperform in its dasha periods.

naisargikaBala.description: Naisargika Bala (Natural Strength) in virupas. Fixed luminosity-based values per BPHS: Sun 60.00, Moon 51.43, Venus 42.86, Jupiter 34.29, Mercury 25.71, Mars 17.14, Saturn 8.57. Invariant across all charts.

planet.description: Planet name. One of the 7 classical Vedic planets (Saptgraha): Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn. Rahu and Ketu are excluded from Shadbala per BPHS.

relativeRank.description: Relative strength rank among the 7 planets (1 = strongest, 7 = weakest). Ranked by strengthRatio (actual/required), not raw virupas, so each planet is compared fairly against its own BPHS threshold.

sthanaBala.description: Sthana Bala (Positional Strength) in virupas. Sum of 5 sub-components: Uchcha Bala (exaltation strength), Saptavargaja Bala (7-divisional friendship), Ojayugma Bala (odd/even sign placement), Kendradi Bala (angular house strength), and Drekkana Bala (decanate gender match). Higher values indicate stronger positional placement.

strengthRatio.description: Ratio of actual Rupas to minimum required (totalRupas / minRequired). Values above 1.0 indicate sufficient strength. Higher ratios mean proportionally stronger planets. Used for ranking planets by relative strength.

totalRupas.description: Total Shadbala in Rupas (totalVirupas / 60). 1 Rupa equals 60 virupas. Rupas are the standard unit for comparing planetary strength against minimum required thresholds.

totalVirupas.description: Total Shadbala in virupas (Shashtiamsas). Sum of all 6 strength components. Higher total indicates a stronger planet in the birth chart. Used for comparing relative planetary strength and evaluating dasha period potential.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

targetDate.description: Date to direct the chart to, in YYYY-MM-DD format. Every natal point is advanced by the solar arc accumulated from birth to this date, about one degree for each year of life.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the birth moment and place used to compute the natal chart and the solar arc.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

directed.description: Every natal point advanced by the solar arc: the planets and bodies in canonical order first, then the Ascendant and the Midheaven.

degree.description: Degree of the directed point within its zodiac sign (0 to 29.999).

directedLongitude.description: Absolute tropical ecliptic longitude after directing, in degrees (0 to 360). Equals the natal longitude plus the solar arc, normalized to a full circle.

interpretation.description: Plain language meaning of this directed point, suitable for chart reports and AI agents. Localized to the requested language.

name.description: Name of the directed point, localized to the requested language. This covers the planets and the two angles, the Ascendant and the Midheaven, alike.

natalLongitude.description: Absolute tropical ecliptic longitude of the point in the natal chart, in degrees (0 to 360).

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the directed point falls in. A directed point crossing into a new sign marks a developmental turning point.

solarArc.description: The solar arc in degrees: the secondary-progressed Sun longitude minus the natal Sun longitude. Approximately one degree per year of life. Every natal point is advanced by exactly this arc.

summary.description: Short overview of the directed chart for previews and report intros. Localized to the requested language.
summary.example: Solar arc directions move every point in your chart forward by the same arc the Sun has travelled by progression, about one degree per year.

targetDate.description: Echo of the date the chart was directed to.

date.description: Transit date in YYYY-MM-DD format (defaults to current date)

natalChart.description: Optional natal chart data to compare transits against

date.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format. A single-digit month or day is accepted and zero-padded (2026-3-5 becomes 2026-03-05). Impossible calendar dates are rejected.

time.description: Time in 24-hour format. Seconds are optional and default to 00 (14:30 becomes 14:30:00); a single-digit hour is zero-padded. Out-of-range values are rejected.

timezone.description: Natal timezone: decimal hours OR IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York"). IANA resolved to the DST-correct offset for the natal date.

time.description: Transit time in HH:MM:SS format (defaults to current time)

timezone.description: Transit timezone: decimal hours from UTC OR IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York"). IANA resolved to the DST-correct offset for the transit date. Defaults to 0 (UTC).

summary.description: Transit aspect summary counts (only included when natalChart is provided). Quick overview of the current transit weather.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in hours used for this calculation.

transitAspects.description: Transit-to-natal aspects (only included when natalChart is provided in the request). Shows which transiting planets are aspecting natal planets.

angle.description: Exact angle of this aspect type in degrees.

interpretation.description: Rich interpretation of the transit aspect including narrative summary, timing, impact assessment, practical guidance, and keywords.

guidance.description: Practical advice for working with or navigating this transit energy.

impact.description: Strength and nature of the transit impact on your natal chart.

keywords.description: Key themes activated by this transit aspect.

summary.description: Narrative interpretation of what this transit aspect means and how it manifests.

timing.description: How long this transit influence lasts based on the transiting planet speed.

isApplying.description: Whether the transiting planet is moving toward exactitude (applying) or away from it (separating). Applying aspects grow stronger.

nature.description: Aspect nature: harmonious (trine, sextile), challenging (square, opposition), or neutral (conjunction).

orb.description: Distance from exact aspect in degrees. Tighter orb = stronger influence.

strength.description: Aspect strength percentage (0-100). Based on orb tightness — 100 is exact.

type.description: Aspect type (CONJUNCTION, OPPOSITION, TRINE, SQUARE, SEXTILE, etc.).

transitPlanets.description: Current positions of all 14 celestial bodies (10 classical planets, lunar nodes, Chiron, Black Moon Lilith) in the tropical zodiac. Use for daily transit tracking, horoscope generation, and aspect monitoring.

degree.description: Degree within the current zodiac sign (0-29.999). Indicates how far into the sign the planet has progressed.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the planet is currently in apparent retrograde motion. Retrograde transits are considered more introspective and revisionary.

latitude.description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees. Near zero for most planets except Moon and Pluto.

longitude.description: Tropical ecliptic longitude in degrees (0-360). Primary coordinate for sign and aspect calculation.

name.description: Planet name (Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto, North Node, South Node, Chiron, Black Moon Lilith). The lunar nodes are the mean node; software using the true node may show node positions up to 1.75 degrees different.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign the planet currently occupies. Changes when longitude crosses a 30-degree boundary.

speed.description: Daily motion in degrees per day. Negative values indicate retrograde motion.

animal.description: Animal symbol associated with this trigram in classical I-Ching imagery and divination.

attribute.description: Core attribute or quality this trigram represents in I-Ching philosophy (e.g., Creative, Receptive, Arousing).

binary.description: Three-digit binary representation of the trigram lines (bottom to top). 1 = yang (solid), 0 = yin (broken).

bodyPart.description: Body part associated with this trigram in traditional Chinese medicine and I-Ching body mapping.

chinese.description: Original Chinese character name of the trigram in traditional script.

direction.description: Compass direction in the King Wen (Later Heaven) Bagua arrangement. Used in feng shui spatial analysis.

element.description: Five element (Wu Xing) correspondence: Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, or Earth. Used in Chinese metaphysics and feng shui analysis.

english.description: English name representing the natural force or element this trigram embodies.

familyMember.description: Family archetype in the Bagua system. Each trigram corresponds to a family role (Father, Mother, First Son, First Daughter, etc.).

meaning.description: Concise interpretation of the trigram covering its symbolic meaning, key associations, and guidance for understanding hexagrams containing this trigram.
meaning.example: The Creative force representing pure yang energy, strength, leadership, and the power of initiative.

number.description: Stable identifier for the trigram, 1 to 8. This is our lookup key, not a canonical sequence: the tradition has several orderings (King Wen, Fu Xi, Earlier and Later Heaven) and they disagree, so do not read ranking or precedence into it.

pinyin.description: Pinyin romanization of the Chinese name with tone marks for correct pronunciation.

quality.description: Energetic quality describing the dynamic nature of this trigram (e.g., Strong, Devoted, Joyous, Gentle).

season.description: Season or time period associated with this trigram in the annual cycle of Chinese cosmology.

symbol.description: Unicode trigram symbol (three lines) for visual display in I-Ching interfaces and Bagua diagrams.

UpagrahaResponse.description: Complete upagraha positions for a birth chart

sunBased.description: Sun-longitude-based upagrahas (Dhuma group). Pure arithmetic from the Sun sidereal position. Dhuma = Sun + 133d20m, then each derived from the previous.

items.description: Position details for a single upagraha (sub-planet)

degreeInSign.description: Degree position within the occupied rashi (0 to 30).

longitude.description: Sidereal longitude in degrees (0 to 360). Used for house placement and aspect analysis.

nakshatra.description: Nakshatra (lunar mansion) the upagraha occupies. One of 27 Vedic nakshatras.

nakshatraIndex.description: Nakshatra number (1 to 27). Ashwini = 1, Bharani = 2, through Revati = 27.

nakshatraPada.description: Pada (quarter) within the nakshatra (1 to 4). Each pada spans 3 degrees 20 minutes.

name.description: Upagraha name. Time-based: Gulika, Mandi, Kala, Mrityu, Ardhaprahara, Yamaghantaka. Sun-based: Dhuma, Vyatipata, Parivesha, Indra Chapa, Upaketu.

rashi.description: Zodiac sign (rashi) the upagraha occupies. One of 12 Vedic rashis from Aries to Pisces.

timeBased.description: Time-based upagrahas derived from the 8-part division of day or night. Gulika and Mandi are from Saturn segment, others from Sun, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter segments. Positions depend on birth time, location, and weekday.

nakshatraPada.description: Pada (quarter) within the nakshatra (1 to 4). Each pada spans 3 degrees 20 minutes.

name.description: Upagraha name. Time-based: Gulika, Mandi, Kala, Mrityu, Ardhaprahara, Yamaghantaka. Sun-based: Dhuma, Vyatipata, Parivesha, Indra Chapa, Upaketu.

rashi.description: Zodiac sign (rashi) the upagraha occupies. One of 12 Vedic rashis from Aries to Pisces.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date determines planetary positions and nakshatra calculations for Vedic kundli (janam patri). Accurate birth date is essential for dashas, yoga calculations, and divisional charts (vargas).

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees. Affects local time calculations and ayanamsha adjustments. Example: Delhi 77.2090, Mumbai 72.8777, Kathmandu 85.3240.

Even minutes matter for accurate nakshatra pada and divisional chart (D9, D10) calculations. Without exact time, Lagna and house-based predictions will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Timezone: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London") OR decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET). IANA strings are resolved to the DST-correct offset for the given date, so you can pass `cities[0].timezone` from /location/search directly. Defaults to 5.5.

birthDetails.description: Echo of the resolved birth data used for detection. Timezone is the numeric offset that the chart engine consumed (IANA names are resolved upstream).

total.description: Count of yogas where present === true in this chart. Range 0-12.

yogas.description: Array of 12 detected yogas. Every entry carries a `present` boolean and a `quality` (Positive, Negative, or Both = auspicious, inauspicious, or context-dependent); filter on present === true for active yogas. Evidence text names the rule that triggered or failed.

description.description: Brief classical formation rule. Identifies the planetary placement, lordship, dignity, or aspect pattern required for the yoga to form.
description.example: Jupiter in kendra from Moon

evidence.description: Human-readable rationale naming the specific rule that triggered or failed the detection, including planetary positions, dignity, kendrādhipati status, lordship, or malefic drishti.
evidence.example: Jupiter in kendra from Moon, not retrograde, no malefic drishti. Strengtheners: Moon out of 6/8/12 (house 4); Moon 5 houses from Sun; Moon not in Scorpio.

id.description: Glossary id (lowercase, kebab-case) matching an entry in the 300-entry planetary-yoga catalog. Use with GET /yoga/{id} to retrieve the full glossary text.

name.description: Classical Sanskrit name of the yoga as referenced in BPHS (Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra), Phaladeepika, and B.V. Raman *Three Hundred Important Combinations*.

present.description: True if every classical condition for the yoga is satisfied by the given chart. False if any rule fails, including "almost-present" cases where dignity is met but kendra/aspect is not.

quality.description: Overall nature. Auspicious yogas (Pancha Mahapurusha, Gajakesari) bestow benefits; inauspicious yogas (Kemadruma) indicate challenges; Both denotes context-dependent effects.

result.description: Classical phala (life-effect) description of the yoga when present, sourced from the parashari and phaladeepika tradition.
result.example: Gajakesari Yoga is one of the most powerful yogas...

apiKey.description: Your API key for accessing RoxyAPI. Alternatively, you can pass the API key as a query parameter "api_key".
apiKey.name: X-API-Key

info.description:
# RoxyAPI: AI-Native Insight Infrastructure

> **Base URL:** `https://roxyapi.com/api/v2`
> All endpoint paths below are relative to this base URL.

The only multi-domain spiritual intelligence API. 12 domains (astrology, Vedic astrology, human design, forecast, biorhythm, tarot, numerology, crystals, dreams, I-Ching, angel numbers, location), 164+ endpoints, one API key, instant activation. Remote MCP server per domain plus AGENTS.md for AI coding agents.

## Who uses RoxyAPI

- **Developers** building astrology apps, tarot platforms, numerology calculators, or dream journals
- **AI agent builders** connecting Claude, GPT, or Gemini to real calculation engines via MCP
- **Vibe coders** shipping insight apps with Cursor, Bolt, or Replit using zero domain knowledge
- **Founders and brands** launching branded spiritual experiences for their audience

## Quick start (60 seconds)

**1. Get your API key** at [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing). Instant delivery, no account required.

**2. Make your first call:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/tarot/draw -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"count": 3}'
```

**3. Monitor usage:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/usage
```

## AI agent integration (Remote MCP)

RoxyAPI ships a Remote MCP server per product over Streamable HTTP, with no local setup and no Docker. Your AI agent auto-discovers all 164+ endpoints as callable tools with zero configuration:
- **Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf**: Add MCP server URL in settings
- **OpenAI Agents, Gemini ADK**: Connect via Streamable HTTP transport
- **Custom agents**: Use the MCP Python/TypeScript SDK

MCP endpoints: `https://roxyapi.com/mcp/{domain}` (e.g., `/mcp/astrology`, `/mcp/tarot`)

Setup guide: [roxyapi.com/docs/mcp](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp)

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key via header or query param:
- **Header (recommended):** `X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY`
- **Query param (testing):** `?api_key=YOUR_KEY`

## Response format

Clean JSON, no wrapper objects. Errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`. The `error` field is human-readable (may change wording). The `code` field is machine-readable (stable — safe to switch on programmatically).

Rate limit headers on every response: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Used`

## Errors

All errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`:

| Status | Code | When |
|--------|------|------|
| 400 | `validation_error` | Missing or invalid parameters. Response includes `issues[]` with per-field `path`, `message`, `code`, `expected`, `minimum`, `maximum`, `format`, `pattern`. |
| 401 | `api_key_required` | No API key provided |
| 401 | `invalid_api_key` | Key format invalid or tampered |
| 401 | `subscription_not_found` | Key references non-existent subscription |
| 401 | `subscription_inactive` | Subscription cancelled, expired, or suspended |
| 404 | `not_found` | Resource not found. Response may include a ranked `suggestions[]` array (each with `endpoint`, `hint`, and a `docs` deep link) for typo recovery. |
| 405 | `method_not_allowed` | Path exists for a different HTTP method. Response includes `allow[]` and the `Allow` header lists valid methods. |
| 429 | `rate_limit_exceeded` | Monthly quota reached |
| 500 | `internal_error` | Server error |

## Pricing

Flat per-request pricing. Every call counts the same, whether a planet position or a full birth chart with aspects. No credit systems, no variable costs. Plans from $39 to $699/month.

See [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing)

## Resources

- [Quickstart guide](https://roxyapi.com/docs/quickstart) - first API call in 60 seconds
- [Documentation](https://roxyapi.com/docs) - guides, tutorials, domain reference
- [MCP setup](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp) - connect AI agents
- [Starter apps](https://roxyapi.com/starters) - clone and deploy in 30 minutes
- [FAQ](https://roxyapi.com/faq) - common questions
- [Contact](https://roxyapi.com/contact) - support and API key recovery
info.title: RoxyAPI

post.description: Get the angel number of the day with full meaning and interpretation. Returns a deterministic angel number based on the current date (or a provided seed date), ensuring all users see the same number for any given day. Includes complete spiritual, love, career, money, and twin flame interpretations plus a biblical perspective and a shadow reading. Perfect for daily guidance features, push notifications, content generation, and angel number widget integrations.

parameters.description: Response language (ISO 639-1). Supported: en, tr, de, es, hi, pt, fr, ru. Defaults to en. Languages without translations yet return English.
parameters.name: lang

schema.description: Response language (ISO 639-1). Supported: en, tr, de, es, hi, pt, fr, ru. Defaults to en. Languages without translations yet return English.

date.description: Date for the reading in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today (UTC). Useful for viewing past daily readings or pre-generating future ones.

seed.description: Optional seed for reproducible readings. Same seed + same date = same angel number every time. Pass any unique identifier (userId, email hash, session token). Omit for anonymous daily readings.

actionSteps.description: Three to five specific, actionable steps to take today based on the angel number guidance. Practical spiritual advice for daily life.

affirmation.description: Positive affirmation aligned with the daily angel number. Use for daily affirmation features, meditation guidance, or spiritual journal prompts.

biblical.description: Biblical and religious perspective on the daily sequence, framed honestly.

coreMessage.description: One to two sentence summary of the divine message for today. Ideal for push notifications, daily guidance widgets, and quick reference.

date.description: The date used for angel number selection (UTC).

digitRoot.description: Numerology digit root calculated by summing all digits and reducing to a single digit. Links the daily angel number to its foundational numerology meaning.

energy.description: Overall energy classification. "positive" indicates encouraging, uplifting energy. "neutral" indicates transitional energy. "cautionary" indicates a gentle warning to rebalance or pay attention.

keywords.description: Five to eight keywords capturing the spiritual themes and energy of the daily angel number. Useful for search, filtering, and content generation.

get.description: Smart angel number analysis that works for ANY number sequence, not just known angel numbers. Automatically classifies the pattern type (repeating, sequential, mirror, master, root, compound), calculates the numerology digit root, checks the database for a known meaning, and provides the foundational digit root interpretation (with full spiritual, love, career, money, and twin flame guidance) as a fallback. An optional context parameter adds a note tailored to where the number was seen. Perfect for synchronicity tracking apps where users enter arbitrary number sequences they encounter.

post.description: Identify classical Western astrology multi-planet configurations in a birth chart. Returns Grand Trines (with element), Kites (with apex), T-Squares (with apex and modality), Grand Crosses (with modality), Yods (Finger of Fate, with apex), Mystic Rectangles, and Stelliums. Each pattern carries a tightness score (0-100), dissociate flag for out-of-sign configurations, and a one-line interpretation suitable for chart reports. Disambiguation is built in: a Grand Cross suppresses its contained T-Squares, a Kite suppresses its underlying Grand Trine. Useful for personalized natal report engines, astrology chatbots, AI agents that interpret chart geometry, and editorial chart-pattern callouts.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Date is critical for house cusp calculations as it determines planetary positions used in some house systems.

houseSystem.description: House system for dividing ecliptic into 12 houses. Placidus (most popular) uses time, Whole Sign (ancient) uses signs, Equal divides from Ascendant. Use "all" to compare all 4 systems side-by-side for educational purposes.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Location determines the local horizon and meridian, which are fundamental to house division. Higher latitudes cause more distortion in time-based systems like Placidus.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Affects local time and horizon calculations for house cusps.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Time is ESSENTIAL for accurate house cusps - even minutes matter. The Ascendant (1st house cusp) changes roughly every 4 minutes. Without accurate time, house placements will be incorrect.

timezone.description: Decimal hours from UTC (e.g. -5 for EST, 5.5 for IST, 9 for JST) OR IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York"). IANA resolved to the DST-correct offset for the chart date.

post.description: Generate a planetary return chart for Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, or Saturn. A planetary return occurs when a transiting planet conjuncts its natal longitude, marking the beginning of a new cycle. Saturn return (~29 years) is the most significant life milestone in Western astrology. Jupiter return (~12 years) signals expansion and growth phases. Mars return (~2 years) resets energy and drive. Returns full tropical zodiac chart with all planetary positions, house cusps, and aspects. Planetary return chart API, Saturn return calculator, Jupiter return chart, Mars return forecast.

approximateDate.description: Approximate date near the expected planetary return (YYYY-MM-DD). Provide a date within the expected return window. The algorithm searches from this starting point.

birthDate.description: Original birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Used to determine the natal longitude of the selected planet.

birthTime.description: Original birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines exact natal planet position for return timing.

houseSystem.description: House system for the return chart. Placidus (default), Whole Sign, Equal, or Koch.

latitude.description: Latitude of the return location in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Affects house cusps and Ascendant of the return chart.

longitude.description: Longitude of the return location in decimal degrees (-180 to 180).

planet.description: Planet for the return calculation. Supports Mercury (~88 days), Venus (~225 days), Mars (~687 days), Jupiter (~12 years), and Saturn (~29 years). Saturn return is a major life milestone in Western astrology.

timezone.description: Decimal hours from UTC OR IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York"). IANA resolved to the DST-correct offset for the birthDate. Output datetime is adjusted to this timezone.

approximateCycle.description: Approximate orbital period for this planet. Mercury ~88 days, Venus ~225 days, Mars ~687 days (~1.9 years), Jupiter ~12 years, Saturn ~29 years.

birthDate.description: Original birth date used for natal planet longitude calculation.

chart.description: Full tropical zodiac chart erected for the planetary return moment. Contains planetary positions, house cusps, aspects, Ascendant, and Midheaven.

angle.description: Exact angular separation that defines this aspect type in degrees.

interpretation.description: Aspect nature. Harmonious (trine, sextile) flows easily. Challenging (square, opposition) creates tension and growth. Neutral (conjunction) blends energies.

isApplying.description: Whether the aspect is applying (planets moving toward exact) or separating (moving apart). Applying aspects grow stronger.

orb.description: Deviation from exact aspect in degrees. Tighter orb means stronger influence.

planet1.description: First planet in the aspect pair.

planet2.description: Second planet in the aspect pair.

strength.description: Aspect strength percentage (0-100). Based on orb tightness relative to the allowed maximum.

type.description: Aspect type. Major: conjunction (0), opposition (180), trine (120), square (90), sextile (60). Minor: semi-sextile, quincunx, semi-square, sesquiquadrate.

birthDetails.description: Birth details used to generate this chart.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

post.description: Calculate a solar arc directed chart for any birth moment and target date. The solar arc is the secondary-progressed Sun longitude minus the natal Sun longitude, about one degree for each year of life, and every natal point including the Ascendant and Midheaven is advanced forward by that same arc. Returns the solar arc, each directed point with its natal and directed longitude, zodiac sign, degree, and a plain language interpretation, for predictive astrology apps timing major life events. Built on accurate tropical chart positions, no astronomy expertise needed.

post.description: Generate a solar return chart for any year, the foundational technique for annual astrological forecasting. The chart is cast for the exact moment the transiting Sun returns to its natal ecliptic longitude (your astrological birthday). Returns full tropical zodiac chart with planetary positions, house cusps, aspects, Ascendant, and Midheaven. Location-sensitive: relocating your solar return chart to a different city changes the houses and Ascendant. Solar return chart API, annual horoscope forecast, birthday chart calculator, yearly astrology prediction, relocated solar return.

Perfect for dating apps, matrimonial sites, relationship counseling tools, and astrology compatibility features. Based on professional astrological techniques.

post.description: Roll the cross-domain forecast for a single birth subject into four pre-summarized windows: the next 24 hours, 7 days, 30 days, and 90 days from the start date. Each window returns its event count, a per-domain count breakdown, a per-type count breakdown, and the top highest-significance events. Built for a glanceable what-is-coming strip so a caller can render the upcoming highlights without scanning the full event list.

parameters.description: Response language (ISO 639-1). Supported: en, tr, de, es, hi, pt, fr, ru. Defaults to en. Languages without translations yet return English.
parameters.name: lang

schema.description: Response language (ISO 639-1). Supported: en, tr, de, es, hi, pt, fr, ru. Defaults to en. Languages without translations yet return English.

birthData.description: The single birth subject this digest is built for. One object only, never an array.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Anchors the natal chart and the Vimshottari dasha sequence.

latitude.description: Birth latitude in decimal degrees. Optional and does not affect the timeline. Defaults to 0.

longitude.description: Birth longitude in decimal degrees. Optional and does not affect the timeline. Defaults to 0.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Precision matters for the natal positions the transit aspects are measured against.

timezone.description: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London", "UTC"), decimal hours (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET), or a fixed UTC offset (e.g. "-05:00", "+01:00"). Prefer the IANA name: it is resolved to the DST-correct offset for the birth date, while a fixed offset or decimal is taken literally and will be wrong if it does not match the daylight-saving state on that date. Invalid timezones return 400 with a validation error.

domainWeights.description: Per-domain significance multipliers applied before the significance floor and event cap. Bias which domains survive filtering and the cap. Omitted domains default to a weight of 1. Valid keys are western, vedic, and biorhythm.

biorhythm.description: Multiplier for this domain significance. 1 leaves it unchanged, above 1 promotes the domain, below 1 demotes it.

vedic.description: Multiplier for this domain significance. 1 leaves it unchanged, above 1 promotes the domain, below 1 demotes it.

western.description: Multiplier for this domain significance. 1 leaves it unchanged, above 1 promotes the domain, below 1 demotes it.

domains.description: Which forecast domains to include before rolling up the windows. Defaults to all three.

items.description: Forecast domain. western covers transit aspects, sign ingresses, retrograde stations, eclipses, and new and full moons. vedic covers Vimshottari mahadasha, antardasha, and pratyantardasha boundaries. biorhythm covers critical days.

minSignificance.description: Drop events scoring below this significance threshold from 0 to 100 before the rollup. Defaults to 0.

startDate.description: Start anchor for every window in YYYY-MM-DD format. The next 24h, 7d, 30d, and 90d windows are measured forward from this date at 00:00:00 UTC. Defaults to today in UTC.

top.description: Number of highest-significance events to surface per window. Defaults to 3, capped at 20.

birthData.description: Echo of the birth subject this digest was built for.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Anchors the natal chart and the Vimshottari dasha sequence.

latitude.description: Birth latitude in decimal degrees. Optional and does not affect the timeline. Defaults to 0.

longitude.description: Birth longitude in decimal degrees. Optional and does not affect the timeline. Defaults to 0.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Precision matters for the natal positions the transit aspects are measured against.

timezone.description: IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London", "UTC"), decimal hours (e.g. -5 for EST, 1 for CET), or a fixed UTC offset (e.g. "-05:00", "+01:00"). Prefer the IANA name: it is resolved to the DST-correct offset for the birth date, while a fixed offset or decimal is taken literally and will be wrong if it does not match the daylight-saving state on that date. Invalid timezones return 400 with a validation error.

endDate.description: Last day of the resolved 90 day horizon the timeline was built over before slicing.

startDate.description: Start anchor every window is measured from.

windows.description: The four rollups in ascending window length: next 24h, 7d, 30d, and 90d from the start anchor.

byDomain.description: Count of events in this window broken down by domain. Only domains with at least one event in the window are present. The values sum to count.

byType.description: Count of events in this window broken down by event type. Only types with at least one event in the window are present. The values sum to count.

count.description: Number of events whose datetime falls inside this window.

days.description: Length of this window in days forward from the start anchor. One of 1, 7, 30, 90.

from.description: Inclusive lower bound of the window as an ISO-8601 UTC datetime, the start anchor.

to.description: Exclusive upper bound of the window as an ISO-8601 UTC datetime, the start anchor plus the window length.

top.description: The highest-significance events in this window, most significant first, up to the requested top count. The same TimelineEvent shape as the timeline endpoints.

aspect.description: For a transit-aspect, the angular relationship. One of conjunction, sextile, square, trine, opposition. Absent for other event types.

body.description: Primary subject of the event. A transiting planet for western events, Sun for a solar eclipse, Moon for a lunar eclipse or a new or full moon, a mahadasha, antardasha, or pratyantardasha label for dasha changes, or the critical cycle for biorhythm days.

date.description: Calendar date of the event in YYYY-MM-DD (UTC).

datetime.description: Exact instant of the event as an ISO-8601 UTC datetime. Astronomical events are refined to this instant by search, not reported at a daily sample point.

description.description: Plain-language summary of the event, suitable for direct display. The only localized field: when lang is set this sentence, and the body, target, and aspect names within it, render in the requested language while the structured fields stay English.
description.example: Transiting Saturn square natal Moon, an exact aspect within 0.12 degrees.

domain.description: Forecast domain. western covers transit aspects, sign ingresses, retrograde stations, eclipses, and new and full moons. vedic covers Vimshottari mahadasha, antardasha, and pratyantardasha boundaries. biorhythm covers critical days. A stable machine value, never localized, so consumers can branch on it under any language.

kind.description: For an eclipse, its classification. total and penumbral apply to lunar eclipses, partial applies to both, annular and total apply to solar eclipses. A stable machine value, never localized. Absent for other event types.

obscuration.description: For a lunar eclipse, the peak fraction from 0 to 1 of the Moon disc covered by Earth umbra. 1 for a total lunar eclipse, between 0 and 1 for a partial, 0 for a penumbral. Absent for solar eclipses and other event types.

orb.description: For a transit-aspect, the separation in degrees from the exact aspect at the reported instant. Tighter orb means a more exact and significant aspect.

phase.description: For a lunar-phase event, which syzygy it is: new-moon (Sun-Moon conjunction) or full-moon (Sun-Moon opposition). The intermediate quarters are not emitted. A stable machine value, never localized. Absent for other event types.

significance.description: Importance score from 0 to 100. Outer-planet exact transit aspects and mahadasha changes score highest; fast Moon events and biorhythm critical days score lower. When domainWeights is supplied this is the weighted score, rounded and clamped to 0 to 100, which is the same value the significance floor and the event cap acted on.

station.description: For a retrograde-station, whether the planet turns retrograde or direct. A stable machine value, never localized. Absent for other event types.

target.description: For a transit-aspect, the natal body the transit aspects. For a sign-ingress, the zodiac sign entered, and for a lunar-phase, the zodiac sign of the New or Full Moon. Absent for other event types.

type.description: Event kind. transit-aspect, sign-ingress, retrograde-station, eclipse, and lunar-phase are western, dasha-change is vedic Vimshottari, critical-day is biorhythm. A stable machine value, never localized, so consumers can branch on it under any language.

200.description: Pre-summarized forecast windows: next 24h, 7d, 30d, and 90d rollups

400.description: Validation error. `issues[]` lists every failed field.

405.description: Method not allowed. The path exists but only responds to the methods listed in `allow[]` and the `Allow` response header.

post.summary: Forecast digest - Pre-summarized next 24h, 7d, 30d, and 90d rollups

post.description: Return only the high-significance dates from the merged cross-domain forecast for a single birth subject: the rare outer-planet exact transit aspects, slow-planet sign ingresses, retrograde stations, and Vimshottari mahadasha and antardasha changes that mark genuine turning points. Defaults to a significance floor of 70 so the response is a short list of the most meaningful upcoming dates. Built for what-is-coming highlights, timing alerts, and at-a-glance forecast strips.
